capital_aquilon.name: Capital Aquilon (Imperial City Aquilon)
capital_aquilon.description: A majestic city, the heart of the Aquilonian Empire. Built on the ruins of a smaller Forerunner city, whose ancient foundations can still be found in the deepest dungeons of the Imperial Palace. A center of power, trade, and imperial magic. Divided into districts: Palace, Merchant, Artisan, Magisterium, and the 'Lower City' slums. Known for its White Tower of the Emperor and the College of Imperial Mages.
current_issues: Growing unrest in the Lower City
legends: Legend of the Sleeping Guardian beneath the city – an ancient Forerunner construct.

whispering_woods.name: Whispering Woods (Sylvanesti Heartland)
whispering_woods.description: A vast, ancient forest in the east, home to elven enclaves of the 'Children of the Forest' (Sylvanesti). Permeated with calm but powerful nature magic. Outsiders are not welcome here, but some elven settlements, like Silverleaf Dell, conduct cautious trade. Deep within the forest lie ruins of even older civilizations and sacred groves with Trees of Wisdom.
points_of_interest: City of Elerion (hidden High Elf capital)
Ruins of the Temple of First Dawn
current_issues: Spread of Ether-corrupted flora and fauna from nearby Ether Scars
Internal disagreements between isolationists and proponents of contact
legends: Legend of the Forest King, sleeping until the hour of need
Tales of will-o'-the-wisps luring travelers into the thicket.

thunder_citadel.name: Thunder Citadel (Khaz Modanar)
thunder_citadel.description: The main stronghold of the Dwarven Mountain Clans, carved into the heart of the Dragon's Claw Ridge. A center of craft, runic magic, and mining of rare metals. A mountain-city extending many miles underground. Houses the Great Forge, where legendary artifacts are created, and the Halls of Ancestors, where dwarven history chronicles are kept.
points_of_interest: Throne Hall of the King under the Mountain
current_issues: Raids by orcs and goblins from the depths
Rivalry between clans for influence and resources
legends: Legend of the Heart of the Mountain – a giant crystal powering the Citadel
Tales of ancient monsters sleeping in the deepest mines.

ruins_arcanum.name: Ruins of Arcanum
ruins_arcanum.description: The ruins of one of the greatest Architect cities, located in a desolate area. A labyrinth of crumbling towers, flooded libraries, and mysterious vaults, filled with traps, automaton guardians, and unstable Ether. Many Seekers of Lost Lore try to find the legendary Central Library of Arcanum here.
current_issues: Activity of competing expeditions (Aquilon, Seekers, cultists)
legends: Legend of the 'Knowledge of All', hidden in the Central Library
Stories of Architect ghosts still roaming the ruins.

ether_scar_chasm.name: Ether Scar: The Bottomless Chasm
ether_scar_chasm.description: A gigantic fissure in the earth, a remnant of the Great Sundering. It emanates raw magical energy, distorts reality, and spawns ethereal monsters. The Chasm's edges are unstable, and its depth is unknown. Rumor has it that gates to other planes or the source of the Sundering itself lie at the bottom. The air here crackles with energy, and the ground is covered with strange crystalline growths.
points_of_interest: Crystallized Forests (petrified trees along the edges)
current_issues: Expansion of the Scar's influence zone
Attempts by the Cult of the Sundered Sky to conduct rituals here
legends: Legend of the 'Heart of the Scar', an artifact of incredible power at the bottom
Stories of those who descended into the Chasm and returned mad or changed.

silver_haven.name: Silver Haven
silver_haven.description: A large port city-state on the southern coast. A melting pot of cultures, a center for free trade, smuggling, and maritime adventures. Ruled by the Council of Merchant Princes. Here you can find goods from all over the world, hire a crew for a sea voyage, or find work without too many questions asked. Famous for its shipyards and bustling taverns.
current_issues: Pressure from the Aquilonian Empire wishing to subjugate the city
legends: Legend of a cursed island with treasures of an ancient pirate king
Tales of giant sea monsters inhabiting the Southern Seas.

floating_islands_of_aethel.name: Floating Islands of Aethel
floating_islands_of_aethel.description: An archipelago of islands held aloft by ancient Architect magic above impenetrable Misty Jungles. A place of mysteries, rare floating crystals (Aerocrystals), Forerunner ruins, and dangerous winged predators. Some islands are connected by rainbow bridges of solidified light. Access is difficult, but the rewards can be great.
points_of_interest: Temple of Winds (central island with control mechanism)
legends: Legend of the 'Beacon of Aethel' – an artifact that can control all islands
Stories of a lost Architect city on the highest island.

nomad_lands_ash_plains.name: Nomad Lands: Ash Plains
nomad_lands_ash_plains.description: Vast, scorched steppes and deserts covered in volcanic ash. Home to nomadic tribes of humans and beastkin (Kharash) adapted to harsh conditions, hunting giant desert creatures (e.g., Sand Worms or Rock Bison), and rare oases. Nomad culture is based on survival, honor, and respect for ancestral spirits and the desert.
points_of_interest: Ancient rock paintings in Ancestors' Canyon
current_issues: Raids by more aggressive tribes or monsters from Ether Scars
legends: Legend of the 'Mother of Oases', a spirit that can lead to water
Tales of an ancient city buried under the sands.

crystal_caves.name: Crystal Caves of the Deep
crystal_caves.description: An extensive underground cave system where Ether has crystallized into huge, energy-pulsating formations of various colors and properties. A source of valuable magical components, but an extremely dangerous place due to unstable energy, crystal golems, and other subterranean creatures. Some dwarven clans (e.g., Deepdelver) have their mines here.
points_of_interest: Heart of the Caves (giant mother crystal)
current_issues: Cave-ins and earthquakes caused by Ether instability
legends: Legend of the 'Living Crystal' that can grant wishes or bestow immortality
Stories of beings made entirely of crystal and consciousness.

forgotten_observatory.name: Forgotten Observatory
forgotten_observatory.description: An ancient Architect observatory atop a secluded and hard-to-reach mountain in the World's Edge Ridge. Rumored to hold knowledge about stars, cosmology, the nature of Ether itself, and possibly the Great Sundering. Protected by complex mechanisms and magical barriers. Many believe it was from here the Architects observed Ethereal Currents.
current_issues: Presence of strange creatures, possibly related to space or other planes
legends: Legend of the 'Map of Destiny' stored in the observatory
Rumors that the observatory can predict future cataclysms or even influence them.

dragon_spine_mountains.name: Dragon's Claw Ridge
dragon_spine_mountains.description: An extensive and high mountain range dividing the western and eastern lands of the continent. Home to the **Thunder Citadel** of the dwarves, as well as numerous orc tribes, giant eagles, and, according to rumor, the last ancient dragons or their descendants. Rich in veins of metals and precious stones. Peaks are often hidden by clouds, and passes are dangerous and impassable in winter.
current_issues: Constant skirmishes between dwarves and orcs
Rumors of an ancient dragon awakening
legends: Legend of the Primordial Dragon whose bones form the ridge
Tales of lost dragon hoards.

sunken_city_of_aeridor.name: Sunken City of Aeridor
sunken_city_of_aeridor.description: Ruins of an ancient Architect city, swallowed by the sea during the Great Sundering. Located in the Azure Gulf south of **Silver Haven**. Its spires and towers are visible underwater on clear days. Attracts treasure hunters and those seeking Forerunner technologies related to water or marine life. Guarded by sea monsters and remnants of defense systems.
current_issues: Curse of the city, driving those who linger too long mad
legends: Legend of the Trident of the Sea Lord hidden in the city
Stories of intelligent sea creatures created by the Architects.

the_scarred_wastes.name: The Scarred Wastes
the_scarred_wastes.description: A vast territory north of the **Ether Scar**, land most affected by the Great Sundering. The landscape constantly changes under the influence of unstable Ether. The most terrible mutants and ethereal elementals inhabit here. Rare daredevils venture here in search of unique ethereal crystals or to hunt particularly dangerous creatures.
points_of_interest: Pulsating Heart of the Wastes (zone of maximum ethereal activity)
Bonefields (remains of those who died during the Sundering)
legends: Legend that the Wastes are slowly consuming the world
Rumors of the possibility to 'cleanse' the land with a powerful artifact.

sanctum_of_whispers.name: Sanctum of Whispers
sanctum_of_whispers.description: A hidden place, usually a cave, ancient temple, or abandoned tower, where followers of forbidden cults, necromancers, or seekers of dark knowledge gather. Its exact location constantly changes or is known only to initiates. Dark rituals are performed here, entities from other worlds are summoned, and the most dangerous aspects of magic are studied.
current_issues: Constant threat from the Order of the Crimson Flame or imperial agents
Internal betrayals and power struggles among cultists
legends: Legend that the Sanctum is connected to the source of all dark magic
Stories of powerful artifacts that can be found or created here.

the_shifting_sands_of_khem.name: The Shifting Sands of Khem
the_shifting_sands_of_khem.description: A vast desert, even more ancient and mysterious than the Ash Plains. Sandstorms here can last for weeks, and ancient ruins of a buried civilization, predating even the Architects, sometimes emerge from beneath the dunes. The nomads of Khem are known for their survival skills and mysterious sand magic. Legends speak of Sleeping Gods beneath the sands.
points_of_interest: Great Pyramid of the Sand Wurm
Ruins of the city of An-Khepri
legends: Legend of the 'Heart of the Desert' – an artifact controlling the sands
Tales of a Sphinx posing riddles to travelers.
